channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Overcoming Payment Barriers for Digital Products in Restricted Countries
item.description: The article describes the challenges of selling digital products in countries where mainstream payment platforms like PayPal and Stripe consider operations 'high-risk.' Initially, the business struggl...

item.title: Mainstream media highlights youth concerns over minor tax changes
item.description: Australia's major media outlets have largely relied on personal anecdotes from young investors to criticise the government’s modest tax reforms, including tweaks to negative gearing, capital gains tax...

item.title: Russian Embassy Responds to Allegations About Africa-Russia Partnership
item.description: The Embassy of the Russian Federation in South Africa has issued a detailed response to the Mail & Guardian opinion piece by Wellington Muzengeza, published on 19 May 2026, which alleged a 'manufactur...

item.title: Calcutta High Court stays coercive action against Abhishek Banerjee in case over remarks on Amit Shah
item.description: The Calcutta High Court on Thursday directed the West Bengal Police not to take any coercive steps against Trinamool Congress MP and national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee till 31st July. This o...

item.title: Supreme Court Recognizes Civil Action for Coercive Control in Intimate Relationships
item.description: The Supreme Court of Canada has acknowledged a civil action for 'coercive control,' marking a significant development in how non-violent abuse in intimate relationships can be addressed legally. This ...

item.title: PM Shehbaz highlights enduring Pakistan-China diplomatic ties on 75th anniversary
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Thursday praised the strong and enduring friendship between Pakistan and China, highlighting that both nations have always supported each other firmly. This statement ...

item.title: Rivers Deputy Governor Urges Couples To Base Marriage On Love And Biblical Values
item.description: Rivers State Deputy Governor, Prof. Ngozi Nma Odu, don advise newly married couples say make dem build their marriage on top love and submission wey Bible teach. She talk this one for the wedding of A...

item.title: Rescue Operations Continue After Boat Capsizes in Lamu County
item.description: Rescue teams in Lamu County are still working hard after a boat capsized at the Kwa Bruno Channel near the LAPSSET corridor. According to the Kenya Red Cross, four people were rescued from the water a...

item.title: Government plans to issue infrastructure bonds to support development
item.description: Tanzania government iko preparing to release infrastructure bonds soon, aimed at boosting funding for major national projects. The bonds will provide investors an opportunity to support initiatives su...

item.title: Murang'a County to Compensate Traders Affected by Protest Damage
item.description: Traders in Murang'a County who lost property and goods during protests over rising fuel prices are set to get some relief from the county government. The demonstrations, which happened on Monday 18th ...

item.title: Former Trump Envoy Advises Caution in Iran Negotiations Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: Former White House spokeswoman and Iran negotiator Morgan Ortagus warned that Iran may be using ongoing nuclear negotiations to delay U.S. pressure while preserving leverage. Speaking at the Middle Ea...

item.title: House Committee Approves Right to Information Bill, Poe Highlights Transparency Importance
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – The proposed Right to Information (RTI) bill is moving closer to becoming law as it clears both the Committee on Public Information and the Committee on Appropriations. Rep. Bria...

item.title: India’s Tea Industry Faces Climate, Economic and Global Pressures in 2026
item.description: On International Tea Day 2026, India’s cherished chai faces multiple challenges despite remaining a cultural staple. The country, one of the world’s largest tea producers and consumers, has seen a sha...

item.title: JAMB Releases 279 Withheld UTME Results After Investigation
item.description: The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) don release 279 results wey dem bin withhold from the recent Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) following some investigations. Accord...

item.title: Israel Starts Deporting Flotilla Activists Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: Israel has initiated the deportation of hundreds of activists involved in flotilla movements, which have historically attempted to challenge the country's naval blockades. The deportations come amid o...

item.title: National Government Confirms Non-Interference in First BARMM Parliamentary Elections
item.description: The national government has assured that it will not interfere with the first parliamentary elections in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM), scheduled for September 14, 2026. ...

item.title: Walmart reports solid first-quarter results amid cautious guidance for the year
item.description: Walmart reported robust sales in its first fiscal quarter, benefiting from its commitment to low prices, faster deliveries, and improved merchandise. Weekly traffic of over 150 million customers demon...

item.title: Lions loose forward Ruan Venter faces five-to-nine-month recovery after serious knee injury
item.description: Lions flank Ruan Venter will miss the remainder of the 2026 season after undergoing knee surgery for injuries sustained early in the United Rugby Championship final round match against Leinster in Dub...

item.title: Eastern Cape Court Orders Government to Ensure Water Access for Centane Residents
item.description: The Eastern Cape High Court has ruled that the Minister of Water and Sanitation, along with the Premier and the Amathole District Municipality, failed in their constitutional duty to provide water to ...

item.title: Muhimbili Hospital Receives Mobile Surgical Unit to Improve Heart Disease Care
item.description: Dar es Salaam, Tanzania — Tanzania is making a big step in healthcare with the arrival of a mobile surgical operation unit focused on cardiovascular diseases. The unit was officially handed over at Mu...
